verdict_reasoning: TGF-beta1 is secreted but forms the Large Latent Complex: LAP remains non-covalently associated with mature TGF-beta1, and the complex is covalently tethered to cell surfaces via disulfide bonds between LAP and GARP (LRRC32) or LRRC33 on Tregs, platelets, and other cells. This wash-resistant covalent attachment to TM partners places latent TGF-beta1 on the outer leaflet — the basis of clinical anti-GARP/TGF-beta1 and anti-LAP antibody programs. Integrin-mediated activation occurs at the cell surface from this tethered pool. Mechanism fits stable_surface_attachment via covalent disulfide tethering to a TM scaffold.
